segment-text: written by Michael Covino & Kyle Marvin

Scenes in this film will be shot as long single takes. While the camera techniques and movement will vary, the scenes will play in real time. Interludes, which serve as transitions between the scenes, will be more stylized and will not always be continuous.
EXT. VILLA - COUNTRYSIDE - DUSK

The song "ALL TOGETHER NOW" by Stonewall Jackson plays. It's a beautiful summer evening. A large family, or soon to be family, is celebrating at a long table. We move across the faces of the family members, all characters that we will meet later; SUZI, JIM, BIANCA, DANI, GRANDMA, UNCLE MARK, GEORGE. The CAMERA lands on KYLE who laughs uncontrollably. The CAMERA cuts to a shot of MIKE. He laughs as well. They are filmed tight and center frame. These are single character portraits. CAMERA on AVA. She laughs until tears well in her eyes. She takes a sip of wine. Kyle leans into the frame to kiss her. CAMERA on Kyle. He sits back into frame. Ava leans on his shoulder. He's happy. CAMERA on Mike. Still laughing. His laughter dies down.
EXT. MOUNTAIN ROAD - FRANCE - DAY

Mike and Kyle ride bikes up an empty mountain road at sunset. The road is quiet except for the spinning mechanics of the bikes and the breath of the riders. Music emanates from Mike's headphones. Hey Mikey?
They pedal on. Mike doesn't hear him. Mike!
Mike looks over and pulls his headphones out.
Can I have the water bottle? Yeah.
Mike reaches down and hands Kyle a water bottle. Kyle takes a swig from the bidon and immediately reacts.
It's called a bidon.
What is this? Orangina.
He hands the bidon back to Mike. Thanks.
No problem.
I mean thanks for this, for setting this up. It's so beautiful. I needed it too, I feel like I've been getting fat.
Yeah. Most people get in shape for their wedding.
Yeah. I didn't do that.
Listen, when we hit the hill try to keep a steady cadence.
What's a cadence?
Your pedal cadence. The rhythm of your feet as they pedal. Just keep
them consistently moving.
So you're like really into this?
Cycling? Yeah. It's fun, and you sweat a lot. Plus it helps me clear my head.
Maybe I'll get a bike when we get back. This could be like our new thing. Yeah.
They ride. The pedals and gears buzz. Mikey.
Mike looks over.
I'm getting married. How awesome is that? Awesome.

She's the best. She's the best person I've ever met. She lets me be myself. I don't have to change. Remember how Marissa made me get that Rob Thomas haircut? And Tina made me be an atheist for a year?
Yeah. I remember that.
Ava isn't like that. I don't have to cut my hair or denounce God. She loves me for who I am. Yeah.
And I love her for who she is. They pedal along.
I can't wait to spend the rest of my life with her-MIKE Kyle I slept with Ava.
Kyle turns to Mike.
I slept with-KYLE --What do you mean slept?
Like I slep... We sexually slept with each other.
Kyle turns the bike around, doing a loop. He comes to a stop.
I'm gonna fucking kill you.
Kyle takes off after Mike, trying to catch up. He pushes the bike as hard as he can.
I fucking hate you so much!
Kyle gains on Mike, he reaches out almost grabbing him, but Mike breaks away. Kyle struggles to keep up.
What?
MIKE
When did this happen?
I don't remember the exact dates but-Dates?
Mike says nothing.
KYLE (CONT'D)
They both pedal in silence, struggling with the climb.
Slow down. You should pace yourself. How long?
Like a quarter mile.
How long has this been going on?
Mike says nothing.
When did it start?
When she moved to New York.
Three years ago? This whole time!?
No. Not the whole time. It was before you started dating. Oh.
A PELOTON of riders pass them at high speed up the hill. They ride in unison. One RIDER shouts at the others in French.
Allez ! Allez ! On y va ! On lache rien !
(translation) Come on! Come on! Let's go! Don't give up!

Mike attempts to keep pace with them. The CAMERA follows. He is quickly exhausted and falls behind, out of breath. The peloton passes. It's quiet again. Mike reaches down to take a sip from his bidon.
Okay. It happened. You had sex. It happened in the past. Yes.
They ride again in silence.
It also happened in the present.
Fuck Mike! (a beat) You're the worst.
He struggles.
You're like a real life Judas.
I know... On the plus side that would make you Jesus. So...
Is that a joke? Are you making a fucking joke?
You're right. They ride in silence.
Kyle. What do you want me to say? I'm sorry!
I'm sorry. I can't explain it. She's just so cool. She's smart and interesting and she does this thing with her hips-
--You're talking about the hip thing now!? She's my fiance, you think I don't know about the hip thing? Sorry.
They breath heavily as the hill kicks their asses.
If I catch you, I'm going to kill you.
I know. That's why I waited for the hill.
Kyle falls behind. They ride again in silence.
Switch gears. Your cadence is too low. Fuck you.
Kyle reaches down to switch gears. The bike makes a loud clanking noise and he swerves. Kyle pedals through his exhaustion.
You got this. Fuck you. Push! Shut up.
KYLE
Mike reaches the top. Kyle pushes as hard as he can. Almost at the top. I'm sorry.
No you're not. I'm sorry.
Kyle struggles to catch his breath as he reaches the top of the hill.
Why are you doing this?
I thought you'd want to know.
Why would I want to know?
I had to tell you. I'm your best man.
Not anymore.
They ride side by side on a flat grade.
I wrote a speech.
I don't care.
They ride.
So who's gonna be your best man?
I don't know. Anyone. Anyone but you can be my best man.
A car lays on its HORN. Mike waves his arm. Go around.
The car honks again.
The car honks repeatedly and then roars past them. Still honking. It swerves back into the lane in front of them, almost hitting Mike.
MIKE (CONT'D)
Mike gets out of his saddle and takes off, pedaling hard.
What are you doing? Hey asshole!
MIKE (CONT'D)

Mike races down the road after the car. The CAMERA stays with Kyle who rides at a slower pace down the road, still out of breath and processing the information. As Kyle comes around a bend in the road, his eyes fix on something happening in the distance. He approaches and the sound of two men screaming gets louder. Kyle slows his bike to a stop. The yelling turns to grunting. The CAMERA swings behind Kyle to reveal Mike being thrown to the ground by the DRIVER of a car that is now pulled over on the shoulder of the road. Kyle does nothing. The driver pushes Mike's face in the dirt. DRIVER Espece de Pignouf!
You hooligan!

Mike flails, trying to break free. The driver punches him in the ribs. Mike gets a hold of the driver's leg and pulls him to the ground. They wrestle. The CAMERA continues moving up the road, passing the window of the car where a LITTLE BOY with blonde hair sits in the passenger seat. The little boy gets out of the car. Papa?
BOY (O.S.)
The driver gets the upper hand. He looks up to see the little boy standing outside the car.
BOY (TRANSLATION) (CONT'D) Are you okay?
The driver's voice becomes soft and paternal. DRIVER Oui mon poussin. Rentre dans la voiture. J'en ai pas pour
Yes my chickadee. Get back in the car. I won't be long.

The little boy gets back in the car and closes the door. The CAMERA continues moving slowly down the road as Mike struggles beneath the man. The driver stands, letting go of Mike. He turns and walks back to his car. He gets in and takes off down the road, passing the CAMERA. Mike rolls on the ground, groaning in pain. Kyle stands beside him, watching. Kyle walks to the edge of the road and stares down. He carefully walks down the embankment. Mike sits up. Kyle climbs back onto the road carrying Mike's bidon.
I found your water bottle.
It's a bidon.
What does that even mean?
I think it's the french word for fancy water bottle.
INT. HOSPITAL - FRANCE - DAY
Mike sits in a hospital room wearing his bloodied cycling regalia. Kyle sits by his bedside, silent.
I think I broke a rib.
I thought you were a good fighter.
Kyle goes to the door and peers into the hallway. The CAMERA stays with Kyle.
These shoes have no traction. Feet are everything in boxing. Why do you think they jump rope?
I don't care.
I'm just saying, imagine if Muhammad Ali had to wear roller skates. Shut up.
Mike shuts up.
Do you love her?
... It doesn't. I'm, this isn't. You're getting married.
Do you love her?
It doesn't matter. She loves you.
What the fuck are you talking about? It matters. I'm supposed to just get married? As if nothing... How do I know it won't happen-MIKE --It won't happen again.
How do I know?
Because it won't.
Swear to me it won't happen again.
A DOCTOR enters the room. He immediately moves to Mike and begins checking him out. He lifts Mike's arm.
I swear. It won't happen again.
The doctor presses on Mike's chest.
DOCTOR
DOCTOR
What's he saying?
He said blue.
(to Doctor) Yes. I can see what color it is. Is it broken?
The doctor grabs his clipboard and begin writing.
Bon, j'ai regarde vos radios.
Vous n'avez rien de casse. Ce
quelques tests car il se peut que vous ayez subit un traumastisme cranien.
Okay. I have looked over your X-Rays. You have nothing broken. It is only bruises. However, I would like to do some tests because you may have experienced some head trauma.
Mike looks at Kyle.
What did he say?
I don't know, he's talking too fast.
Est-ce que vous me comprenez? Comprenez-vous le Francais?
Are you understanding me? Do you understand French?
Oui. J'etudie le francais au lycee.
Yes. I study the French in high school.
The doctor slows his speech.
Okay. (slowly)
Il a des bleus severes mais il n'a rien de casse. Pouvezvous dire ca a votre ami?
Okay. He has some severe bruising but nothing is broken. Can you tell that to your friend?
Il n'est pas mon ami.
He's not my friend.
(under his breath) Ah, putain...
(under his breath) Ah, fuck!
The doctor turns to exit.
DOCTOR (TRANSLATION) (CONT'D) Follow me.
Kyle doesn't understand.
Come. Come.

The doctor waves and Kyle follows him out of the room. The CAMERA turns back to the room. Mike is alone. He leans forward and sees himself in the mirror; bloodied. He takes a phone out and takes a picture of himself.
Hey.
The camera turns to reveal a woman at the door, this is AVA. AVA Where's Kyle?
With the doctor.
Ava walks away leaving the doorway empty. Mike sits alone. Ava reappears.
I don't see him. Is he okay?
Yeah he's fine. I mean, emotionally he's not.
She looks at him.
I told him, about us.
She processes this but shows no reaction.
And he beat you up?
No. A Frenchman beat me... he didn't beat me up. My feet were slipping and I think he used Krav
Why are you doing this? I had to.
Mike we talked about this. It's over between us.
Right, that's what I told him. You love each other. What I did was... what we did was reckless and impulsive and meaningless.
I agree. It meant nothing to me as well.
Okay. So that's it. You're gonna marry him.
Yes. I'm going to marry him.
I'm happy for you.
Tears begin to well-up in Mike's eyes. Me too.
It's silent.
I thought a lot about our story.
They look at each other.
It didn't start good. It means something-MIKE I love you.
It's quiet.
I'm sorry. I shouldn't have said that... Please don't tell Kyle.
You're such an asshole. Putain! Fais chier merde!
What does that mean?
It means fuck you make me shit. I'm sorry.
(IN FRENCH) You fucking say this now. Right before my marriage. Fuck.
What are you saying?
I don't know. I don't fucking know.
They move closer together, Mike trying to console her. They touch. Moving closer. They kiss.
What the fuck!? You just promised!
The CAMERA comes around Mike and Ava to find Kyle standing in the doorway looking on. He turns and leaves. Ava goes after him.
Kyle, wait!
The CAMERA follows her. Kyle!
AVA (CONT'D)
He doesn't respond. She chases him down the hall and finally catches him by the arm. He turns around. Neither of them know what to say.
Can we talk?
About what?
About this.
No. I don't want to talk about this. I fucking hate this! Okay.
No! I do want to talk about this! Tell me what's happening. Okay.
Do you still love me? Yes.
Okay. Do you still want to get married?
She hesitates.
Okay, maybe we're rushing this conversation. We're all worked up. Let's talk about it tomorrow.
The doctor approaches them, followed by a NURSE.
Venez, venez. Suivez moi.
Come, come. Follow me.
Follow us please.
The doctor and nurse usher Kyle and Ava back into Mike's hospital room. The doctor gestures to the nurse. Anais?
Yes, hi. The doctor has asked me to translate for him. You're American yes? Yes.
Je suis Francaise. NURSE

Nous avons regarde ses radios, tout a l'air d'aller bien. Toutefois j'aimerais faire quelques tests pour voir s'il a subit un traumatisme cranien, mais il ne montre aucun signe de commotion. Il a de severes bleus mais rien de casse. Je vous conseille de mettre de la glace.

We have looked over his XRays and everything appears to be okay. I would like to test for head trauma, but he shows no immediate concussion symptoms. His body is bruised, but nothing is broken. My suggestion is to ice it.
He says. The X-rays are good. They show no issues. He does not think you have a concussion but he wants to test your head. And you have bruise so you must ice them.
I'm pretty sure I broke my ribs. NURSE (to the doctor)

segment-speaker: Il dit qu'il s'est casse une
segment-text: cote.
(to the doctor) He says he broke a rib.
Non, il n'a rien de casse. Ses cotes sont intactes.
No, he did not. His ribs are intact.
He says your ribs are not broken.
Look at this.
Mike lifts his shirt. He has a massive bruise on his ribs.
Oh god Mike. Bleus.
Bruise.
I know it's blue.
Ava touches the bruises softly.
No he says it's bruised.
Why are you touching him?
Ava pulls her hand from Mike.
He's fine! He has a bruise. Can we just go back to the hotel and get some sleep and then tomorrow we can wake up fresh, get married and never talk about this again.
Ava looks back at Kyle.
I can't get married.
Kyle goes quiet. The room goes quiet. And then. Ahhh!
Kyle punches Mike in the bruised ribs.
Owwww!! Fuck!
Mais qu'est ce qu'il se passe?
What's happening?
You broke it. NURSE Elle est supposee se marrier avec lui mais je crois qu'elle est amoureuse de celui avec les bleus.
She's supposed to marry him. But I think she's in love with the bruised one.
The doctor nods.
(to Nurse) Pouvez-vous arretez ca?
(to Nurse) Can you not do that?
(to Kyle) He, ce n'est pas grave. Cela fait parti de la vie. C'est
rend plus fort. Lorsque un os se casse, il est inutile et faible mais lorsqu'il se repare, le cal grandi autour de la fracture. Et le cal devient encore plus fort que l'os ne l'a jamais ete.

(to Kyle) It's okay. This is life. It is often painful, but we grow stronger as a result. When a bone breaks it is weak and useless. But as it heals, callus grows around the fracture. And that callus becomes stronger than the bone ever was.
Putain mais de quoi parlez vous?
What the fuck are you talking about?
What did he say?
He said. This is life. And when a bone breaks it is...
Arretez. Assez. Pourquoi
dites vous ces conneries?
Stop. Enough. Why are you saying this shit?
What did he say about broken bones?
(to Ava) Vous etes dans un hopital. Je
suis medecin. Je ne fais que
professionnel.
(to Ava) You are in a hospital. I am a doctor. I am just giving my professional opinion.
Es-tu serieux?
Are you serious?
Oui. Je suis un docteur. Dans la vie, les gens se blessent.
Yes. I am a doctor. In life people get hurt.
What is he saying?
He says. People get hurt. And he is a doctor. Fuck you!
(to doctor) Fuck you.
EXT. CEMETERY - DAY

Men's voices sing the Ricky Van Shelton version of the hymnal "I SHALL NOT BE MOVED" sung a capella. The CAMERA looks at the sky and trees, moving through a lightly wooded area. The CAMERA, still moving, drops to reveal it's a cemetery. The CAMERA stops, a tractor scoop bucket drops down into the frame. The CAMERA moves down with the bucket as it tears into the ground, digging a grave. A CEMETERY WORKER continues to sing "I shall not be moved" near a dug grave. The CAMERA moves around the dug grave to reveal five more WORKERS singing while resting and eating. They are all wearing GREEN JUMPSUITS that read, "Eternal Hills Cemetery".
EXT. CEMETERY - FUNERAL - DAY
A PRIEST speaks in front of a group of mourners.
For the Lord himself will descend from heaven with a cry of command, with the voice of an archangel, and with the sound of the trumpet of God.
BEEP. BEEP. BEEP. A LOUD BEEPING NOISE of a tractor in reverse can be heard in the distance. He pauses to let it pass. The beeping stops.
And the dead in Christ will rise first.

It starts again. BEEP BEEP BEEP. The CAMERA leaves the priest and wanders past the mourners toward a tractor moving in reverse in the distance. The tractor stops. The CAMERA turns to find a man in the distance, walking toward camera. As he gets closer we see it is Kyle. He wears a suit.
As Kyle gets closer, the CAMERA begins leading him back in the direction of the funeral.
GROUP (O.S.)
Kyle enters the crowd of mourners. He walks past a girl who notices him and nods. As he passes she whispers to the guy next to her.
And now her husband Michael would like to say a few words.
Kyle watches on. His face is stern, emotionless.
Thank you all for coming. Thank you father for the beautiful words. It's strange hearing them because I know that she didn't really believe in heaven.
The CAMERA turns to reveal Mike is giving the eulogy.

She was more into the idea of reincarnation but this all happened so fast and I didn't even know where to begin finding a priest who would talk about reincarnation. So I just went with the basic package that they sold me at the funeral home.
He gets choked up.
But thank you father for being here. I'm sorry Ava.
He stares at the coffin. Beside the grave is a framed picture of Ava.
I think I fucked this up. Can we just get it over with?
Mike grabs a shovel and lifts some dirt. He's about to throw it on the grave when a CEMETERY WORKER in a green jumpsuit reaches out and grabs the shovel.
Sorry, you can't do that. It's a Union cemetery.
I'm burying my wife.
I understand, but if you're not Local 365 you can't touch the equipment.
It's a fucking shovel.
ANOTHER CEMETERY WORKER steps closer. Mike tries to pull the shovel away from the man but he doesn't let go. Instead he rips it from Mike's hands. Fuck you!
MIKE (CONT'D)
The priest calls out to Mike.
Michael. It's okay.
Okay. Sorry father.
Mike turns away, then quickly lunges for the shovel, ripping it from the worker's hands. The worker goes after the shovel, but Mike swings it, keeping him at a distance.
Back up. Stay back. I'm gonna throw some dirt on this grave, and then I'm gonna go home.
Put the shovel down. NO!
MIKE
Mike swings the shovel wildly to keep the cemetery worker back. He accidentally hits the large glass framed picture of Ava. It shatters and goes crashing to the ground.
Oh no. Ava!
Mike turns around and the two cemetery workers grab and wrestle him to the ground. The CAMERA turns back to Kyle who watches on. The crowd does nothing. Kyle rushes toward the fight.

Kyle pulls one of the men off of Mike. He grabs Mike by the hands and pulls him away from the other man, dragging Mike across the cemetery lawn to a safe distance. The CAMERA follows them. Kyle lets go. Mike rolls onto his stomach and lies face down on the grass. Kyle watches on.
Hey. It's okay. Come on.
Kyle reaches down and lifts Mike up by the chest from behind. Mike stands. Kyle lets go and he immediately falls back to the ground.
Okay come on. Let's get up.
Kyle lifts him again. This time holding him up, embracing him from behind. The CAMERA stays on them as they embrace. Mike turns his head and notices it's Kyle.
Hey.
KYLE
Thanks for coming... I didn't think you were gonna come. Yeah.
Kyle lets go of Mike. Mike stays on his feet and turns to face Kyle. Kyle stares at him for a moment then turns to leave.
I miss her.
Kyle turns back to Mike. I get it.

Yeah. You're probably the only person who gets it. How special she was. How amazing... but also frustrating she could be. You know she started rescuing dogs? At one point we had six dogs living with us. And one of them really didn't like me. I'd wake up in the middle of the night to go to the bathroom and Gus would just growl at me. I mean these aren't like normal rescues these are like street dogs... But that's the thing, we built this life together and it wasn't perfect but it was ours. And it worked... She was patient and loving. Everyone talks about how when you get married the sex gets worse... but it just got better, like so much better...
The CAMERA comes around to reveal they are within earshot of the funeral crowd who are listening.
Hey, let's just, come here.
Kyle leads him away from the crowd, out of earshot.
It's just, she was my partner, and now she's gone.
Kyle says nothing.
You're my best friend and I know I lost that. But now I lost my wife, so I'm realizing how important best friends are. And I'm sorry. They hug.
I'm not sorry for being with her, because I loved her. But I'm sorry for what it did to us, and...
Kyle walks away. Mike is left standing alone. Kyle steps back in front of Mike. Here.
Kyle holds out a shovel. Mike takes it. Kyle walks away again. Mike stands holding the shovel. The CAMERA stays with him. A tractor roars to life in the background.
Hey! Get off that tractor! Make me!
KYLE (O.S.)

The cemetery workers run toward the tractor. They pass Mike. Mike walks back to the grave site. BEEP. BEEP. BEEP. The sound of the tractor echoes across the cemetery, mixed with the sound of men yelling at Kyle. Mike jams the shovel into the ground and pours dirt onto the grave. He digs it into the ground again.
INT. SPIN CLASS - DAY

Lights are low. Music blasts. The CAMERA moves across the room, passing faces. Sweaty faces. Beautiful sweaty faces. A female SPIN INSTRUCTOR shouts over the music but we never see her. She can be heard faintly in the background but the music is at the forefront. Not everything she says will be
Yes! Todd! Keep pushing!
More good looking people pedal hard on their stationary bicycles.

Each person the CAMERA passes is more sexy than the last. They move up and down perfectly to the rhythm. Then we see Kyle. He does not move to the rhythm. Sweat pours from his face and he looks like he might pass out at any moment. Kyle flicks his hand up to wipe the sweat pouring from his forehead. His sweat whips into the face of the woman riding next to him. She turns her head to Kyle. He mouths "sorry" then smiles. She turns forward and keeps pedaling. This is MARISSA.
And get ready we're gonna get out of the saddle in three, two, one. Let's go. Third position.
Kyle sees Marissa stand as she pedals. He tries to follow.
Marissa taps her butt back to the rhythm. Kyle tries to tap to the rhythm but he's way off.
Kyle turns and stares at Marissa. Marissa looks straight ahead pedaling at a fast pace. She's very good at this. Kyle picks up his pace, pedaling as fast as he can.
INT. BASEMENT - FAMILY HOUSE - THANKSGIVING - DAY

Kyle sits on a bed. He is much skinnier and has a trendy new haircut. This is his childhood bedroom. The intro to "LULLABY" by Shawn Mullins plays. Kyle moves to the song. His eyes close. He's feeling it. The CAMERA moves tight on his face.
She grew up with the children of the stars. In the Hollywood Hills
and the boulevard.
Kyle mouths the words seductively. He looks up, focusing on someone offscreen.
Her parents threw big parties, everyone was there. They hung out with folks like Dennis Hopper, Bob
Seeger, Sonny and Cher.
Kyle stands. Dancing. The CAMERA pulls back, giving him room to move and dance.
Now, she feels safe. In this bar on Fairfax. And from the stage I can tell that she can't let go and she can't relax.
He pulls his shirt off seductively.
And just before she hangs her head to cry I sing to her a lullaby. I sing everything's gonna be alright Rockabye. Rockabye.
He undoes his belt.
Everything's gonna be all-The music cuts out. Kyle looks up, confused and still in his moment.
Hey! I was doing a whole seduction thing.
I can see that.
I thought you thought it was funny.
The CAMERA swings around to reveal MARISSA, smoking a cigarette out of a cracked basement window.
It was funny, when you were fat. Now it feels serious. Oh.
Are you gonna talk to them?
Yeah. It's Thanksgiving. I'm gonna talk to everyone.
She smiles.
Kyle, I'm fucking serious.
I know. I will. If we make a thing out of it, then it becomes a thing.
It is a thing.
Kyle's mom screams down from upstairs.
Kyle, turkey's ready!
You need to tell her.
Kyle yells back.
Alright mom, one second.
Your mom doesn't like me.
My mom loves you.
She didn't like me in high school.
That's not true. You shouldn't smoke down here though, she can smell it.
Kyle. This turkey needs to be cut!
Marissa puts the cigarette out.
Yeah. Hold on!
Just tell her no.
But I'm the turkey cutter.
Have you ever said no to your mother? Yes.
I honestly don't think you have. Right.
Let's practice. Say it to me. Say no. No.
I don't believe it. Let's try this. Kyle, can you drive me to the airport?
Sure. I mean no. Unless you actually needed a ride, cause...
Okay. Let's try again. We're just saying no. Kyle can you take me to the airport? ... no.
Here, ask me something.
Okay. Can you take me to the airport?
No! Absolutely not.
Kyle stares at her.
Did you believe me? Yes.
Good. Can you take me to the airport? No.
No! You need to mean it. No!
NO! Say it again! No! NO!
Their faces get closer to each other. NO!
Kyle can you take me to the fucking airport? NOOOOO!!!!
Marissa kisses him.
That was really good.

She smiles and pulls him onto the bed. They make out. Marissa slowly guides Kyle's head down between her legs. The CAMERA moves away from them and through the basement to a staircase. It heads up the stairs toward an open door. At the top of the stairs, Suzi stands peering down the staircase listening intently.
Mom, what are you doing?
Suzi turns around. The CAMERA moves past her and into the kitchen.
INT. KITCHEN - FAMILY HOUSE - THANKSGIVING - CONTINUOUS
DANI and BIANCA, Kyle's sisters, are standing in the kitchen. Other FAMILY and EXTENDED FAMILY members are scattered around the house. I think they're fighting. Suzi's voice has a hint of excitement.
You need to give them privacy.
It's my house.
An electric knife begins buzzing in the background.
Dani, we don't like her.
Bianca starts making manhattans in a shaker.
Wait, why don't we like her again? Because.
(shouting) Kyle. Your father's cutting the turkey!
Who wants a manhattan? Everyone raises their hands. Everyone.
Grandma you want a manhattan? Of course.
GRANDMA (O.S.)
Suzi walks over to Jim who is struggling with cutting the turkey.
The CAMERA pans to see Kyle standing in the doorway. He's a bit disheveled and out of breath.
What are you doing? You don't cut straight down the middle! You have to start with legs and thighs.
Kyle walks quickly over to his father. The CAMERA follows.
It was getting cold. Jim hands Kyle the electric knife. Kyle's sister Bianca continues making manhattans.
You want a manhattan? Of course.
Kyle begins carving the turkey, starting with the legs. Bianca hands Suzi two manhattans. Suzi walks over to Kyle and sets one of them down next to him.
Everything okay? Yeah, why?
I don't know, it sounded like you were fighting down there.
No, we're good.
He slices expertly.
If you guys are going through a rough patch-Mom. Stop.
Okay. Sorry. I know you've been through a lot. But I didn't like her when you were together in high school and then you followed her to --I chose to go to Oswego.
You got into Amherst.
We're not having this conversation.
She broke up with you your first semester.
--Kyle! Your commercial is on!
